events.description: Join us at the library this summer and watch a movie on the big screen! Bring a friend, and a snack! Rated PG September 1 - The Cat in the Hat (2003). Run Time: 1h 22 min. Rated PG September 4 - Ratatouille (2007). Run Time 1h 51 min. Rated G.

events.title: Yoga Storytime with Appleseed Yoga
events.description: ​Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Appleseed Yoga for Kids will deliver inspiring stories that get the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materials required. Drop-in with limited space. All

events.title: Learn Not to Burn: Fire Safety for Children
events.description: Join the Toronto Fire Service in this special visit to teach our young community members about fire safety and prevention, and what to do when there is an emergency. Drop-in program for children and families. No registration required, space is limited.

events.title: Under the Sea BINGO
events.description: For ages 6 to 12.  Children under the age of 6 may attend but must be accompanied by a parent or caregiver.   Dive under the sea and celebrate the end of the summer with a game of ocean-themed BINGO! Kids will enjoy multiple rounds of play with underwater-themed cards, friendly competition and chances to win fun book prizes each round.

G) August 27 - Muppet Treasure Island (1996, 99 min. G) For children ages 6 and up. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Philippe Flahaut: Sous le soleil de l’été

Perfect for young artists of all skill levels 8-10 years old.

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. events.description: Do you like to build with LEGO, DUPLO, Feltro, Snap Circuits or KEVA? Join us every Saturday morning for some fun building challenges! All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Scratch Coding for Kids (8+)
events.description: Learn scratch coding and create a game. We’ll explore the fundamentals of block coding while completing a basic project. No prior knowledge of coding or scratch interface is required to attend. To save project a scratch account must be made prior to the class. ( https://scratch.mit.edu/ ). Parents must be able to log their child into the scratch account. Scratch account is NOT mandatory to participate.  All materials will be provided. Registration is required and is limited to ages 8+.

events.title: Sunday Funday
events.description: Join us for a fun afternoon of video games, board games, Lego and ping pong. Drop in, no registration required. Recommended for ages 6-12.

events.title: Sunday Family Crafts
events.description: Come as a family and enjoy making crafts together. This program is for families or caregivers with children. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Craft Stations
events.description: Join us this summer for a fun afternoon filled with crafts! We will have various craft stations set up; you can go to one or all! This program is suitable for all ages; younger children must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Drop in until space is full.

events.title: Movie Monday:  Elemental (2023)
events.description: Join us to watch the film and enjoy making a themed craft or colouring. In Element City, fire, water, land, and air residents live together. The story introduces Ember, a tough, quick-witted, and fiery young woman whose friendship with a fun, sappy, go-with-the-flow guy named Wade challenges her beliefs about the world they live in. Age Rating: PG Run Time: 1h 49m For Families. Drop in.

events.title: Summer Library Bingo
events.description: Join us for an hour of bingo fun. Prizes and fun for all. Drop in.

events.description: Join us for some TD Summer Reading Club Fun! We will have stories, crafts, and other activities based on this year's TD Summer Reading Club theme. For children ages 4-12. Drop-in, registration is not required.

events.description: Have you ever wondered what it would be like to be an inventor? Have you ever thought about building something yourself? What is something you would like to build? Is there a problem you would like to fix? Use your imagination and brainstorm creative solutions to develop a machine that will fix a problem or make a task easier for you! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 4 to 8 with their parents/caregivers. Registration starts on Tuesday, August 4 at 9 am.

events.title: Learn Not to Burn: Fire Safety for Children

events.title: Under the Sea BINGO
events.description: For ages 6 to 12.  Children under the age of 6 may attend but must be accompanied by a parent or caregiver.   Dive under the sea and celebrate the end of the summer with a game of ocean-themed BINGO! Kids will enjoy multiple rounds of play with underwater-themed cards, friendly competition and chances to win fun book prizes each round.

events.title: Summer Movies

G) August 27 - Muppet Treasure Island (1996, 99 min. G) For children ages 6 and up. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.title: Summer BINGO
events.description: For children ages 6-12. Cool off and play a few rounds of Summer Bingo for fun and the chance to win cool books, stickers and more. No registration – but ticket is required. Space is limited. Tickets will be handed out at the 2 nd floor desk 15 minutes before the program starts.

events.title: Fictional Character Bingo and TD Summer Reading Club Draw
events.description: Let's finish off this summer with a fun round of Bingo! This time, we'll be doing popular fictional characters in lieu of numbers. Have you read lots of stories? Maybe you can guess who they are too! Prizes will be given out for Bingo winners! Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 4 to 8. Space is limited. Registration begins June 1st. Information Collection Notice: The perso

Hands on Exotics, an exotic animal shelter, will introduce a variety of furry, feathery, and scaly animals while sharing fascinating facts about wildlife, conservation, and responsible pet ownership. Bring your teddy bears and blankets and listen to the classic story, sing some songs and participate in fun activities! Weather permitting this program will take place at Hubbard Park (550 Gerrard Street East) or inside the Riverdale branch. For children of all ages with their parents or caregivers. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.title: 3D Design and Print Painting For Kids Part 2 (Print Painting)
events.description: Join us for Part 2 where kids can unleash their creativity painting 3D printed models that they had created during Part 1. This hands-on class will introduce children to the world of 3D printing in a playful and engaging way. Come ready to have a blast! For more information about this program please contact dihonline@tpl.ca

events.description: Create, build, and invent with Makedo cardboard construction tools! events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: When The Cavas Speaks-Artistic Journeys by Sarah Chi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Sarah Chi are on display at Northern District Library branch in an exhibit. Sarah is her school’s Art Club Head, a TPL Teen Council and Young Voices Council member. She has created art since age six and began expressing personal ideas at fourteen. events.title: Field of Dreamers: Commemorating the Christie Pits Riots

Registration is not required. Drop-in attendance is available until room capacity is reached.

events.title: Income, Expense and Budget
events.description: Understanding how money flows in and out of your household is the foundation of good financial health. Presented by YMCA-GTA, this session, we’ll break down different types of income, common expenses, and how to create a realistic and effective budget. You’ll learn simple, practical tools to track your finances, plan for savings, and avoid common budgeting pitfalls. This workshop will help you take control of your money and work toward your financial goals with confidence. Drop in, no registrati

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. See how an image can be altered and repaired using patch tools such as the clone and heali

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! Presented by Marlowe Andreyko from Dino Bites. This program is for ages 9+ .  Drop-in, no registration required.

events.description: Join us for a relaxed session of games and good company. We’ve got chess sets and a great selection of board games ready to play. Whether you're into strategy or just here for some laughs, all skill and interest levels are welcome! Drop-in (no registration required).

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Cricut Mugs
events.description: Turn a plain mug into a masterpiece! Join us for a hands-on workshop where you will learn to use Cricut machine to design, cut, and press your very own custom mug. Space is limited. Please arrive at 3:30pm to secure your spot in the program. Participants must be available both dates to ensure completion of project.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.
